ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: *REPOSTED 5/23/24 9:09 am* Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Need IRS written satisfaction and release as paragraph #10 indicates. 2. Need an authority under which the court can grant Sarah Lum's shares to be directly distributed to her trust. No Exhibit A & B (to the Sarah's Trust) was not provided. Attorney Dawkin's oral argument appears insufficient. Also, an authority for the court to grant the alternative distribution to the County. 3. Need info if Betty Wong had taken $700 monthly fees during her tenure of special admin in 2018. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CRAIG STEVEN PORTER SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/9/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The petition caption does not reflect that the conservator is requesting reduction in bond. [CRC 7.102.] (3) Attorney costs have no description of what was rush filed and returned or delivered, etc. In particular, the register of actions does not reflect any filing on 6/1/22, and it is unclear what was delivered on 5/22/23 and 5/31/23. There are two identical entries of $46.31 on 1/1/23. This Court does not ordinarily permit reimbursement for research. What were the research charges for on 3/31/23 and 6/30/23? (4) Why is rent not being increased each year? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUSTAVO P. SERAFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/18/2024 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) Notice must be given to the trustee of the devisee trust. If the trustee consented to electronic service, the consent must be filed on the mandatory Judicial Council form (EFS-005-CV). 2) No financial account statement has been filed to verify the cash balance as of 01/25/2024. 3) Paragraph 9 of the petition refers to the conservatee's "Fidelity" retirement account, but no such account has been inventoried and appraised. 4) Paragraph 12: If the conservator's attorney spent 263.85 hours, the total should be $118,732. 5) Paragraph 13: If the conervatee's attorney spent 41.3 hours, the total should be $14,455. 6) Paragraph 14: Was the estate the designated beneficiary of the conservatee's Transamerica annuity? 7) How should the insufficient cash on hand be allocated among the conservator, conservator's attorney, and conservatee's attorney in partial satisfaction of their respective fees and costs? 8) Schedule A does not show a $4.56 dividend credited to the IRA account 8116 on 10/28/2021. 9) Schedule C shows a $10,129.73 disbursement to the conservatee's unsupervised trust on 11/15/2021. Did the trust advance expenses of the estate? 10) Schedule C shows a $443.39 disbursement to the probate referee on 01/03/2024, but according to the second supplemental inventory and appraisal, the referee's commission was $433.39. 11) Schedule D shows a sale of Prudential shares after the conservatee's death, but no prior court approval appears to have been obtained for the liquidation. See Probate Code section 2631(b).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NENA M. LIOTTA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/2/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Paragraph 13 of the petition states that there were no changes in capital (?) assets, but at least one of the securities held in the account xxx6120 is no longer listed as being on hand. (2) The total market value of assets on hand is not what is distributed: the specific net amount of cash and the specific securities must be set forth in the prayer and in the proposed order. (3) If there is $27,142.75 in cash on hand as of 10/31/23 (plus the JP Morgan MMF of $1,984.82), the Court cannot order "cash in the sum of $189,037.94." (4) The balance of the statutory compensation is presumably being waived by the attorney and the executor? Please provide a courtesy copy of the amendment to the petition that addresses these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/18/24. A revised proposed order that also orders distribution of net cash and specific securities (with just the number of shares, not the fluctuating FMV) must also be provided. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTHER HAEJI JUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/2/2024. The issues have not been cleared: (1) Proof of publication. The proof on file 4/8/24 does not show complete information that was published. Unless a proof of publication showing that Prob. Code sec. 8120 is met, republication will be needed. This issue has not been cleared since the initial hearing on 3/26/2024. If petitioner wishes to withdraw the petition, petitioner may file a written request. (2) Proposed order to be submitted. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Authority To Remove Nonresident"S Property To Place Of Residence
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/25/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 04/29/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged (one previously uploaded was rejected by the clerk). 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form on each financial institution at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be verified. The verification must include language required by CCP section 2015.5. 4) Each translation certificate must include language required by CCP section 2015.5. 5) The German court's certificate (consisting of the court's decision and letters of guardianship) with the original apostille(s) must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. 6) The German court clerk's certification appears to have been made in 2016. Do the German Court's decision and letters of guardianship issued in 2016 continue to remain in effect on the same terms and conditions? 7) Please identify specific "financial management" powers the petitioner as guardian/conservator is granted under applicable German law with respect to the US bank accounts and annuity. 8) A copy of the application for the Wells Fargo account 1021 identifies the petitioner as a co-applicant. Is the petitioner a registered joint owner of the account? Please provide a copy of a statement of the account or other documentation showing how title is currently held. 9) Re the Wells Fargo accounts 7529 and 7593 titled under the name of the ward/conservatee's trust: i) Please provide a copy of the trust. ii) Is the petitioner the sole trustee currently acting? If not, please provide the names and addresses of all trustees to whom notice must be given. iii) Is the petitioner requesting the Court's determination that the accounts beneficially belong to the ward/conservatee individually, despite legal title held by the trustee(s)? If so, has the petitioner filed a petition under Probate Code section 850(a)(1)(D) or (a)(3)(A) by opening a new trust case (PTR) under the name of the trust?
